improvementNotes: Keep XR / Stereo rendering: Disable XR Plug-in Management for this build target if this is not an XR project.
Keep URP Debug Display: Safe to strip in shipping builds unless you intentionally ship debug rendering tools.
Keep Editor Visualization: Usually safe to strip from player builds.
Keep DOTS Instancing: Keep only if using Entities Graphics or custom DOTS instanced rendering. GPU Resident Drawer is tracked separately.
Keep URP GPU Resident Drawer: Keep if URP GPU Resident Drawer is enabled. Otherwise disable GPU Resident Drawer and BatchRendererGroup variants in Graphics/URP settings.
Keep Adaptive Probe Volumes: Strip only when the target does not use Adaptive Probe Volumes and relies on lightmaps, reflection probes, classic light probes or no GI.
Keep Screen Space Lens Flare: Remove Lens Flare (SRP) components and renderer features if this target does not use screen-space lens flares.
Keep Foveated Rendering: Safe to strip for non-XR targets. Review if XR stereo keywords or XR packages are detected.
Keep URP Water System: Strip when the Water Renderer Feature / water components are not present in the selected build target.
Keep Procedural Instancing: Keep if you use DrawMeshInstancedIndirect, GPU instancing systems or procedural vegetation.
Keep GPU Instancing: Usually keep. Strip only if you are absolutely certain no material uses GPU Instancing.
Keep URP Additional Lights: In every URP Asset used by Graphics and Quality, set Additional Lights to Disabled if your game uses only the main directional light plus baked lighting.
Keep URP Additional Light Shadows: Disable Additional Light Shadows in all URP Assets, or reduce the number of shadow-casting point/spot lights.
Keep URP Main Light Shadows: Keep for most 3D projects. Strip only for fully unlit or fully baked scenes with no realtime directional shadows.
Keep URP Soft Shadows: Disable Soft Shadows in all URP Assets if hard shadows are acceptable.
Keep Light Cookies: Remove cookie textures from lights and disable cookie support if you do not use projected light patterns.
Keep Light Layers / Rendering Layers: Disable Light Layers / Rendering Layers in URP Assets if lights do not target specific rendering layers.
Keep URP Decals: Remove Decal Renderer Features and Decal Projectors if the game does not use decals.
Keep Screen Space Ambient Occlusion: Remove the Screen Space Ambient Occlusion Renderer Feature if not used.
Keep Reflection Probe Options: Disable Reflection Probe Blending and Box Projection in URP Assets if not needed.
Keep Lightmaps / Mixed Lighting: Keep if any scene uses baked lighting, mixed lighting, probes or shadow masks.
Keep Fog: Disable fog in Lighting/Environment and remove runtime fog changes if the game does not use fog.
Keep Motion Vectors: Keep if you use motion blur, TAA, DLSS/FSR-like systems or effects requiring motion vectors.
Keep LOD Cross Fade: Disable Animate Cross-fading on LODGroups if you do not need dithered LOD transitions.
Keep Native Render Pass: Keep if your URP renderer uses native render pass features.
Keep Screen Coord Override: Use Project Settings > Graphics stripping options for screen coord override variants where possible.
Keep URP Anti-aliasing / Post-process AA: Keep the AA keywords used by UniversalAdditionalCameraData or Volume Profiles; strip unused AA modes per build target.
Keep Temporal Upscaling / TAA / FSR hooks: Keep if cameras use TAA, temporal upsampling, FSR-like upscalers or any custom pass that depends on motion vectors.
Keep SpeedTree vegetation: Keep when materials reference SpeedTree shaders or vegetation packages. Strip only for builds with no SpeedTree assets.
Check Project Settings > Graphics > Always Included Shaders. Remove broad shaders such as URP/Lit or large Shader Graph master shaders unless absolutely required.
Enable Unity's built-in shader stripping options in Project Settings > Graphics and URP Asset settings before relying on custom stripping.
Make sure every enabled Quality level for your build target uses compatible URP feature settings. One quality level with extra features can force extra variants.
Use a Shader Variant Collection for warmup and tracking, instead of putting large shaders in Always Included Shaders.
Addressables and AssetBundle-marked assets are scanned when enabled, but Resources, generated bundles and runtime-created materials can still be missed. Keep review keywords conservative.
Shader Variant Collections are treated as keep evidence because Unity uses them to preload or preserve variants that may not appear in scenes.
Review Renderer Data Inventory when using Forward+ or Deferred; _ADDITIONAL_LIGHTS_VERTEX pressure is primarily a Forward renderer concern.
Review Volume Profiles for Motion Blur, TAA and temporal upscaling before stripping motion-vector or post-processing keywords.
Review Compute Shader keyword pragmas separately from graphics shader material keywords; compute variants can be kept alive by URP features such as SSAO and screen-space passes.
Use the Always Included Shaders auditor to remove catch-all shaders that force broad variant inclusion.
Use the SRP Batcher tab to fix shader layout problems that affect runtime performance even when variant counts are reduced.
